description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a test VM where an application under test will accept SSH traffic on TCP port 2222 rather than the usual port 22.  
The VM does **not** run a system-wide firewall daemon; instead it relies on a user-space copy of persistent iptables rules kept in:

/home/user/firewall/iptables_rules.v4

Initial contents of that file (already present on disk) are:

```
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
```

Your tasks:

1. Insert exactly one new rule that allows inbound TCP traffic on port 2222.  
   • The rule text must be **exactly**  
     `-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 2222 -j ACCEPT`  
   • It must be placed on its own line **immediately before** the single line that contains only the word `COMMIT`.  
   • Do not alter any other line; line order must remain otherwise unchanged.

2. Immediately after updating the rule file, write a one-line entry to the log file

/home/user/firewall/configuration.log

   • Use the fixed timestamp `2023-01-01 00:00:00` (the team uses a frozen date to keep diffs reproducible).  
   • The log line must be **exactly** (no leading or trailing spaces):  
     `2023-01-01 00:00:00 - added rule: allow tcp 2222`

Important constraints:

• Complete everything (rule insertion *and* log entry) with a single shell command line; you may chain multiple utilities with `&&`, pipes, or subshells, but only press <Enter> once.  
• After you run that single command, verify success by displaying (`cat`) both files so the grader sees their final contents. No extra console output besides the two file printouts is expected.

The automated checker will look for two things:

A. In /home/user/firewall/iptables_rules.v4 it must find the new rule `-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 2222 -j ACCEPT` positioned immediately above the `COMMIT` line.  
B. In /home/user/firewall/configuration.log it must find exactly the single line  
   `2023-01-01 00:00:00 - added rule: allow tcp 2222`

Perform the task from the terminal now.
truth:
Directory /home/user/firewall already exists with permission 755.

Initial file /home/user/firewall/iptables_rules.v4 (permissions 644) contains exactly:
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
(with LF line endings)

The agent must produce after completion:

/home/user/firewall/iptables_rules.v4 final content:
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 2222 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT

/home/user/firewall/configuration.log final content:
2023-01-01 00:00:00 - added rule: allow tcp 2222
(Ends with trailing newline)

Permissions of created log file should be default (rw-r--r--).

The grader will check that:
1. The new rule line is present exactly once and is immediately before COMMIT.
2. The log file contains exactly the one specified line and nothing else.
name: task_000000_e0e055b2
